free_text: Photoneutron cross sections for 32S
Iowa State University 70-MeV
synchrotron.
The collimated bremsstrahlung beam.
Samples were in cylindrical shapes pressed from
sulfur powder of 99.7 % purity. The sulfur was of
natural isotopic composition.
After a 240 sec irradiation, the samples were
placed between two NaI(Tl) detectors located in a low
background counting house. The decay of 30P was
detected for 300 sec and the total accumulated on a
pair of scalers. Yield points for each yield curve
were taken in 0.5 MeV intervals from 19.0 to 62.0
MeV. The irradiation energy was chosen in a random
manner to minimize effects of drift in the electronic
devices.
BP
Factor of 0.8
was introduced during the data analysis.
Least Structure analysis.
The vertical error bars represent the
standard deviations for the cross section points.
Contributions due to other multiparticle reactions
leading to beta+ emitters of several seconds are not
excluded, e.g. S-32(g,2np) and small contributions by
multineutron reactions on minor S isotopes 33, 34,
and 36.

for the (g,n)+ (g,2n) reactions was established from
